text.title: AI Research Tool Categories Taxonomy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-research-tool-categories methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Taxonomy MUST recognise four canonical categories: live-web-search, citation-graph, document-qa, synthesis-over-uploaded.
rule.rationale: These four cover the dominant 2025-2026 AI research tool patterns; ignoring one creates blind spots.

rule.statement: Each category MUST be tagged with its corpus bias (web-recency / academic / vendor docs / user uploads).
rule.rationale: Corpus bias drives where the tool is and isn't reliable; unmarked it leads to mis-trust.

rule.statement: Tool-selection workflow MUST pick category FIRST, then product within the category.
rule.rationale: Jumping straight to a product picks by familiarity, not fit; category-first cuts that bias.

rule.statement: Each category MUST be tagged with a typical budget band ($/mo) so finance can pre-approve.
rule.rationale: Without budget bands, procurement cycles delay selection by weeks.

rule.statement: If team is already aligned on stack OR running single-shot research, skip the taxonomy.
rule.rationale: Taxonomy is an orientation artefact; redundant once orientation exists.
